documentation.value:
Outputs the given strings to standard output.
```bash
echo "Hello World"
echo -n "No newline"
echo -e "Line1\nLine2"
```
Do not output the trailing newline.
Enable interpretation of backslash escapes (`\n`, `\t`, etc.).
One or more strings to output.

Reads a line of input and splits it into variables.
```bash
read -p "Enter name: " name
read -r line < file.txt
read -t 5 -p "Quick! " answer
```
Do not treat backslashes as escape characters.
Display `prompt` before reading input (without trailing newline).
Time out and return failure after `timeout` seconds.
Use `delim` as the line delimiter instead of newline.
Variable name(s) to assign input to. Defaults to `REPLY` if omitted.

Formats and prints data according to a format string.
```bash
printf "%s is %d years old\n" "Alice" 30
printf "%05d\n" 42
```
A format string using `%s` (string), `%d` (integer), `%f` (float), `%x` (hex), etc.
Arguments to substitute into the format string.

Evaluates a conditional expression and returns 0 (true) or 1 (false).
```bash
test -f /etc/passwd && echo "File exists"
[ -d /tmp ] && echo "Directory exists"
[ "$a" = "$b" ] && echo "Equal"
```
A conditional expression: `-f file`, `-d dir`, `-z string`, `-n string`, `s1 = s2`, `n1 -eq n2`, etc.

Declares variables and sets attributes.
```bash
declare -i num=42
declare -a arr=(1 2 3)
declare -A map=([key1]=val1 [key2]=val2)
declare -r CONST="immutable"
```
Declare an indexed array variable.
Declare an associative array variable.
Give the variable the integer attribute.
Make the variable readonly.
Mark the variable for export to child processes.
Variable name with optional initial value.

Creates a local variable within a function.
```bash
my_func() {
  local name="Alice"
  local -i count=0
  echo "$name"
}
```
Variable name with optional initial value, scoped to the current function.

Marks variables for export to child processes.
```bash
export PATH="/usr/local/bin:$PATH"
export MY_VAR="hello"
export -n MY_VAR  # unexport
```
Remove the export property from the variable.
Variable name with optional value to export.

Reads and executes commands from `filename` in the current shell.
```bash
source ~/.bashrc
source ./config.sh arg1 arg2
. ./env.sh
```
The file to source (execute in current shell).
Optional positional parameters passed to the sourced script.

Defines actions to execute when the shell receives a signal.
```bash
trap 'echo Interrupted; exit' INT TERM
trap 'rm -f /tmp/lockfile' EXIT
trap '' SIGINT  # ignore SIGINT
```
Command string to execute when signal is received. Use `''` to ignore, `-` to reset.
One or more signal names or numbers: `EXIT`, `INT`, `TERM`, `HUP`, `ERR`, `DEBUG`, etc.

Sets or unsets shell options and positional parameters.
```bash
set -euo pipefail
set -x  # enable debug tracing
set +x  # disable debug tracing
```
Exit immediately if a command exits with non-zero status.
Treat unset variables as an error.
Print each command before executing (debug mode).
Set option by name: `pipefail`, `noclobber`, `nounset`, etc.

Shifts positional parameters to the left by `n` (default 1).
```bash
while [ "$#" -gt 0 ]; do
  echo "$1"
  shift
done
```
Number of positions to shift. Defaults to 1.
A string of option characters. A colon after a character means it takes an argument (e.g. `"f:v"`).
Variable name to store the current option character.
Optional arguments to parse instead of positional parameters.
